data.title: CHAPTER 5 — THE FIVE SUPERVISORY CAPACITIES
blocks: This chapter gives a working overview of The Five Supervisory Capacities, focusing on the ideas a reader needs before mo
Chapter 5 — The Five Supervisory Capacit — These are the five things you do that Claude cannot. Name them. Exercise them explicitly. Never delegate them.

data.title: OPENING
blocks: Seth was mid-build on the grading tool. He had asked Claude to draft feedback for a stack of ten essay submissions. The
Opening — The drafts were fluent. Each one identified the essay's thesis, named one or two specific strengths, noted one or two ar

data.title: THE FIVE CAPACITIES.
blocks: · Feedback that describes the essay structure correctly but in language that does not match what you would write to this s
A draft that praises a strength the student has been working on for a month and the praise is generic enough that the student will not feel seen.
· Output that respects the rubric in form (touches each criterion) and not in intent (treats each criterion as equal weigh

data.title: HOW THE CAPACITIES FIRE ACROSS ONE GRADING-TOOL BUILD.
blocks: Open Claude Code in the grading project. Run /init if you have not already. Read the CLAUDE.md. choice this is a CLAUDE.md project, not a Skill project; the rubric will live in CLAUDE.md because every grading session uses i

Compose the specification for "draft feedback for one student." Single submission. Single rubric. Five elements. for the feedback per submission, not pattern across submissions, on this assignment because the assignment is short and the per-

Run in plan mode, edit via Ctrl+G, approve. Claude executes. Returns the draft. on the draft does the feedback match what I would say to this student? on the tone: does the encouragement land for this student who

The draft is close. Two of three rubric criteria are addressed; the third is dropped. again the dropped criterion is the one the student needs feedback on most. reminds: the spec said all three criteria must be a
/rewind. Restore to before the failed step. revert-and-respecify, not forward-correction.

S05.meta: // SLIDE 05 — DIAGNOSING A FAILED BUILD BY A

data.title: DIAGNOSING A FAILED BUILD BY ABSENT CAPACITY.
blocks: · PF? The spec was "generate feedback for each submission." No mention of avoiding cross-submission pattern leakage. Vague
· PA? Each draft was read once before sending. The pattern leakage was not obvious in a fast scan. PA fired weakly because
· TO? The tool was a single Claude prompt that processed all 23 submissions in one session. The context window saturated;
· IJ? The teacher knew the students well enough that, on a slower read, the wrong-essay feedback would have been caught. T
· EI? The spec from three weeks earlier said each draft is independent. The current execution violated this. EI did not fi
